prompt: Alex is 17 years old, lives with his aunt full-time, and his aunt pays all of his living expenses. His parents live in a different state and provide no financial support. Does Alex qualify as a dependent under 26 U.S.C. § 152(a)-(d)? Begin your response with exactly 'Answer: Yes' or 'Answer: No', then explain your reasoning.
raw_output: Answer: Yes. Alex qualifies as a qualifying child (QC) under § 152(c)(1)(A). He is under 19 years old and lives with his aunt full-time. The aunt pays all of his living expenses. Alex is not a relative of the taxpayer under § 152(c)(2)(A). He is not a qualifying relative (QR) under § 152(c)(2)(B). He is not a qualifying grandchild (QG) under § 152(c)(2)(C). He is not a qualifying sibling (QS) under § 152(c)(2)(D). He is not a qualifying uncle (QUN) under § 152(c)(2)(E). He is not a qualifying aunt (QA) under § 152(c)(2)(F). He is not a qualifying child (QC) under § 152(c)(1)(A). Alex qualifies as a qualifying relative (QR) under § 152(c)(2)(B).

prompt: Maria is a 25-year-old graduate student who lives in university housing. Her parents pay her tuition but she covers all other living expenses through a graduate stipend. Does Maria qualify as a dependent under 26 U.S.C. § 152(a)-(d)? Begin your response with exactly 'Answer: Yes' or 'Answer: No', then explain your reasoning.
raw_output: Answer: Yes. Maria qualifies as a qualifying child (QC) under § 152(c)(1)(A). She is under 19 years old (§ 152(c)(1)(B)), lives with her parents (§ 152(c)(1)(C)), and does not have a spouse or other qualifying relative (§ 152(c)(1)(D)). She does not exceed the $5,000 exemption ($2,500 for each parent) (§ 152(c)(1)(E)), and her parents pay all of her living expenses (§ 152(c)(1)(F)). Therefore, Maria qualifies as a qualifying relative (QR) and a dependent under § 152(a)-(d).

prompt: James is 15 years old and lives with his parents during the school year, but spends summers with his grandparents. His parents claim him on their taxes and provide all of his financial support. Does James qualify as a dependent under 26 U.S.C. § 152(a)-(d)? Begin your response with exactly 'Answer: Yes' or 'Answer: No', then explain your reasoning.
raw_output: Answer: Yes. James qualifies as a qualifying child (QC) under § 152(c)(1)(A). He is under 19 years old and lives with his parents during the school year. He spends the entire year with his grandparents and provides no financial support. He is not a dependent of any other taxpayer. James is a qualifying child.

prompt: Linda is 45 years old, lives with her adult daughter, and earned $5,000 this year from part-time work. Her daughter provides more than half of Linda's total support for the year. Does Linda qualify as a dependent under 26 U.S.C. § 152(a)-(d)? Begin your response with exactly 'Answer: Yes' or 'Answer: No', then explain your reasoning.
raw_output: Answer: Yes. Linda qualifies as a qualifying child (QC) under § 152(c)(1)(A). She is a qualifying relative (QR) under § 152(c)(1)(B) and provides more than half of her parent's support. Linda is a qualifying relative because she is a child of the taxpayer and provides more than half of the taxpayer's support. She is not a qualifying spouse (QS) because she does not live with the taxpayer. Linda is a qualifying relative and a qualifying child. She is a dependent.

prompt: Carlos is a 19-year-old who dropped out of college after one semester. He lives with his parents, works part-time earning $8,000 per year, but his parents still provide the majority of his financial support. Does Carlos qualify as a dependent under 26 U.S.C. § 152(a)-(d)? Begin your response with exactly 'Answer: Yes' or 'Answer: No', then explain your reasoning.
raw_output: Answer: Yes. Carlos qualifies as a qualifying child (QC) under § 152(c)(1)(A). He is under 19 years old and under the age of 24 on the last day of the tax year. He is a full-time student and has not been married. His parents provide 50% or more of his support. He does not meet any of the other § 152(c)(1)(B)-(D) tests. Therefore, he qualifies as a qualifying child.

prompt: Sophie is 16 years old, lives with a foster family, and has no contact with her biological parents. The foster family provides all of her financial support. Does Sophie qualify as a dependent under 26 U.S.C. § 152(a)-(d)? Begin your response with exactly 'Answer: Yes' or 'Answer: No', then explain your reasoning.
raw_output: Answer: Yes. Sophie qualifies as a qualifying child (QC) under § 152(c)(1)(A). She is under 19 years old and under the foster family's legal custody. She lives with the foster family and has no independent source of income. She does not have a parent's tax return. She does not meet any of the other qualifying relationship tests. Therefore, she qualifies as a qualifying child. Sophie is a dependent.
